title: toxicodendron acuminatum
intro: Toxicodendron acuminatum, also known as the urushiol oak or Japanese lacquer tree, is a plant species native to East Asia that belongs to the family Anacardiaceae. The sap of this plant contains urushiol, a toxic resin that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Toxicodendron acuminatum include treating skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis, as well as reducing fever and relieving pain. In traditional herbal preparations, the sap is often extracted and used to make lacquer, a protective coating that has been used for centuries in Japanese art and furniture-making.
